senses.definition: A large geological structure made of several connected anticlines that together form a broad arch in rock layers.
examples: The mountain range is part of a huge anticlinorium that formed millions of years ago.
Geologists mapped the anticlinorium to understand how the rocks were folded.
Oil exploration companies study the anticlinorium for possible reservoirs.

senses.definition: Plural of anticlinorium: several large geological structures made of connected anticlines forming broad arches in rock layers.
examples: The region contains several anticlinoriums separated by deep valleys.
Different anticlinoriums can show very different rock ages.
These anticlinoriums were created by the same plate collision.

senses.definition: Variant spelling of anticlinoria: plural of anticlinorium, meaning large geological arch‑like fold regions made of several anticlines.
examples: Several anticlnoria can be traced across the entire orogenic belt.
The mapped anticlnoria show the direction of compressive forces.
Older rocks are exposed in the cores of the anticlnoria.

senses.definition: In the opposite direction to the hands of a clock in most countries.
examples: Turn the handle anticlockwise to open the valve.
The dancers moved slowly anticlockwise around the fire.
Rotate the knob anticlockwise until it stops.

senses.definition: Moving or arranged in the opposite direction to the hands of a clock in most countries.
examples: The anticlockwise motion of the storm surprised the forecasters.
Set the machine to anticlockwise rotation for this part of the cycle.
The planets orbit in an anticlockwise direction when viewed from above the North Pole.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent a pipe, filter, or other passage from becoming blocked.
examples: The new razor has an anticlogging design that is easy to rinse.
Engineers developed an anticlogging filter for the irrigation system.
This anticlogging nozzle reduces maintenance needs.

senses.definition: Trying to stop blood from forming clots.
examples: The patient is taking anticlotting medication after surgery.
Doctors monitor the effect of anticlotting drugs with regular blood tests.
This anticlotting therapy lowers the risk of stroke.

senses.definition: In an awkward, clumsy, or ugly way.
examples: He moved anticly across the small stage.
The furniture stood anticly in the crowded room.
She laughed anticly, unsure of how to react.

senses.definition: The front part of the shin, especially the front edge of the tibia.
examples: He suffered a sharp blow to the anticnemion during the game.
The armor plate protects the anticnemion from injury.
Pain along the anticnemion can signal a stress fracture.

senses.definition: Playful, silly, or strange behavior like that of an antic person.
examples: His anticness kept the children laughing all afternoon.
There is a touch of anticness in her performance.
The film’s anticness may annoy some viewers but delight others.

senses.definition: A misspelling or rare variant of anticoagulant, meaning a substance that prevents blood from clotting.
examples: The label mistakenly listed the drug as an anticoagulan.
In this old text, 'anticoagulan' is used instead of 'anticoagulant'.
She asked whether an anticoagulan was the same as a blood thinner.

senses.definition: A drug or other substance that stops blood from clotting or makes it clot more slowly.
examples: The doctor prescribed an anticoagulant to reduce the risk of stroke.
Patients on anticoagulants need regular blood tests.
Some animals produce natural anticoagulant substances in their saliva.

senses.definition: Preventing or slowing the clotting of blood.
examples: He is receiving anticoagulant therapy after his operation.
Smoking can reduce the anticoagulant effect of aspirin.
Anticoagulant properties make this compound useful in surgery.

senses.definition: Plural of anticoagulant: drugs or substances that prevent blood from clotting or make it clot more slowly.
examples: Anticoagulants are often prescribed to people with irregular heartbeat.
These new anticoagulants require fewer blood tests than older ones.
Some anticoagulants are taken by mouth, while others are injected.

senses.definition: To stop blood from clotting or make it clot more slowly, usually by using a drug.
examples: Doctors anticoagulate patients before certain heart procedures.
The sample must be anticoagulated to allow proper testing.
He was anticoagulated immediately after the diagnosis of deep vein thrombosis.

senses.definition: Present participle of anticoagulate: stopping blood from clotting or making it clot more slowly.
examples: Anticoagulating the blood reduces the chance of another heart attack.
The nurse is anticoagulating the patient in preparation for surgery.
We are anticoagulating samples to prevent clot formation.

senses.definition: Having the effect of stopping blood from clotting.
examples: An anticoagulating agent is added to the test tubes.
This anticoagulating solution keeps donated blood usable for longer.
Researchers tested several anticoagulating compounds.

senses.definition: The process of stopping blood from clotting or making it clot more slowly, especially by using drugs.
examples: Long‑term anticoagulation is required after some heart valve surgeries.
The guidelines discuss when to start and stop anticoagulation.
Poor control of anticoagulation can lead to bleeding problems.

senses.definition: Having the power to stop blood from clotting or to slow clotting.
examples: The plant extract shows anticoagulative activity in lab tests.
Researchers are studying its anticoagulative properties.
This anticoagulative effect can be dangerous in high doses.

senses.definition: A substance or device that prevents blood from clotting.
examples: The lab uses an anticoagulator in the blood collection system.
Heparin is a commonly used anticoagulator in hospitals.
The venom acts as a powerful anticoagulator.

senses.definition: A natural substance produced by some animals that stops blood from clotting.
examples: Leeches secrete anticoagulin to keep the host’s blood flowing.
Researchers isolated anticoagulin from mosquito saliva.
Anticoagulin helps blood‑feeding insects feed for longer periods.

senses.definition: A group of three bases on a tRNA molecule that matches and binds to a codon on mRNA during protein production.
examples: Each tRNA has a specific anticodon that pairs with a codon on mRNA.
The anticodon ensures that the correct amino acid is added to the growing protein chain.
Mutations in the anticodon can change which amino acid is carried.

senses.definition: Plural of anticodon: several groups of three bases on tRNA molecules that match codons on mRNA.
examples: Different tRNAs carry different anticodons and amino acids.
The set of all anticodons allows the cell to read the genetic code.
Researchers study how anticodons evolve across species.

senses.definition: Opposed to careful thinking or reflection.
examples: The policy has an anticogitative effect, discouraging critical debate.
Some see social media trends as anticogitative forces in modern life.
His anticogitative comments shut down further discussion.

senses.definition: A method in physics and electronics where a signal is recorded only if another signal is not present at the same time.
examples: The detector uses anticoincidence to reduce background noise.
Anticoincidence circuits help identify true events in particle experiments.
They added an anticoincidence shield around the main detector.

senses.definition: Intended to prevent or protect against cold or a cold illness.
examples: She bought an anticold jacket for the winter trip.
The company markets anticold tablets for the flu season.
The anticold campaign encourages people to wash their hands often.

senses.definition: Used to relieve or prevent colic pain, especially in babies.
examples: The doctor recommended an anticolic syrup for the infant.
She bought anticolic drops at the pharmacy.
This anticolic formula is gentle on the baby’s stomach.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent vehicles, aircraft, or other moving objects from crashing into each other.
examples: Modern cars often include anticollision systems as standard.
The plane’s anticollision lights were clearly visible at night.
Engineers tested the anticollision software in a simulator.

senses.definition: The act or technology of preventing collisions.
examples: Anticollision has become a key feature in self‑driving cars.
The study focuses on anticollision in busy shipping lanes.
Improved anticollision reduces accidents and saves lives.

senses.definition: Opposing colonial rule or control by a foreign power.
examples: The country saw a rise in anticolonial movements in the 20th century.
He wrote an anticolonial novel criticizing the empire.
Anticolonial activists organized protests across the region.

senses.definition: A person who opposes colonial rule or control.
examples: Many anticolonials were imprisoned for their beliefs.
As an anticolonial, she supported independence movements around the world.
The conference brought together anticolonials from several continents.

senses.definition: Belief in or support for ending colonial rule and control by foreign powers.
examples: Anticolonialism shaped many independence movements in the 20th century.
Her research explores the literature of anticolonialism.
Anticolonialism challenged the ideas that justified empire.

senses.definition: Different forms or versions of belief in or support for ending colonial rule.
examples: The book compares various anticolonialisms across the globe.
These anticolonialisms differ in their use of violence.
Post‑war anticolonialisms changed the political map of the world.

senses.definition: Relating to opposition to colonial rule or control.
examples: He is known for his anticolonialist speeches.
Anticolonialist writings inspired a generation of activists.
The museum presents an anticolonialist view of history.

senses.definition: A person who opposes colonial rule or control.
examples: He became a leading anticolonialist in his country.
Many anticolonialists were exiled for their activities.
The conference honored several famous anticolonialists.

senses.definition: Plural of anticolonialist: people who oppose colonial rule or control.
examples: Anticolonialists organized protests in major cities.
Many anticolonialists later became national leaders.
The meeting brought together anticolonialists from different colonies.

senses.definition: Plural of anticolonial: people or groups that oppose colonial rule.
examples: Anticolonials joined forces with labor activists.
The museum highlights the work of local anticolonials.
These anticolonials challenged the empire’s authority.

senses.definition: Opposition to forming combinations, alliances, or unions, especially in politics or business.
examples: The law reflects a strong anticombination attitude toward big business.
Anticombination was common in early antitrust thinking.
Their anticombination policy blocks any merger talks.

senses.definition: Located on the opposite side of a body from a comet or from where a comet appears.
examples: The anticomet point lies directly opposite the visible comet in the sky.
Astronomers calculated the anticomet position for the observers.
The phenomenon could be seen only from the anticomet side of the Earth.

senses.definition: A statement that attacks or rejects a previous comment.
examples: Her post was quickly followed by an angry anticomment.
The article sparked a wave of anticomments on social media.
He deleted several harsh anticomments from his blog.

senses.definition: Not interested in profit or mass sales, or opposing commercial values.
examples: The band took an anticommercial approach to their music.
His anticommercial attitude surprised the investors.
The film’s anticommercial style made it hard to market.

senses.definition: Opposition to putting profit and commercial interests above other values.
examples: Anticommercialism is strong in certain art movements.
Her anticommercialism led her to refuse advertising deals.
The book explores anticommercialism in modern culture.

senses.definition: Different forms or expressions of opposition to commercial values.
examples: The essay compares the anticommercialisms of various subcultures.
These anticommercialisms share a distrust of big corporations.
New anticommercialisms have emerged with digital media.

senses.definition: A person who opposes strong commercial values or consumer culture.
examples: As an anticommercialist, she avoids brand sponsorships.
Anticommercialists criticize the influence of advertising.
The group is made up of artists and anticommercialists.

senses.definition: Showing opposition to commercial values or profit‑driven culture.
examples: The magazine has a strongly anticommercialist tone.
Their anticommercialist stance attracts a niche audience.
He gave an anticommercialist speech about art and money.

senses.definition: Strongly opposed to commercialism or the pursuit of profit in art, culture, or society.
examples: The movement has an anticommercialistic spirit.
His anticommercialistic views put him at odds with the industry.
They adopted an anticommercialistic approach to publishing.

senses.definition: The quality of not being focused on profit or commercial success.
examples: The gallery prides itself on its anticommerciality.
Anticommerciality can make it hard for artists to earn a living.
There is a certain anticommerciality in their publishing model.

senses.definition: In a way that avoids or opposes commercial or profit‑focused aims.
examples: The project is run anticommercially, relying on volunteers.
They chose to release the album anticommercially, without advertising.
The brand behaves anticommercially despite growing success.

senses.definition: The state or quality of being opposed to commercialism or profit‑driven goals.
examples: There is a certain anticommercialness in their work.
Her anticommercialness appealed to independent audiences.
The festival’s anticommercialness sets it apart from others.

senses.definition: Opposition to communism as an economic or political system.
examples: Anticommunism was a powerful force during the Cold War.
The party’s platform is based on strong anticommunism.
Her book examines anticommunism in post‑war Europe.

senses.definition: Different forms or versions of opposition to communism.
examples: The article describes the various anticommunisms of the 20th century.
These anticommunisms range from liberal to fascist.
Scholars compare Western and non‑Western anticommunisms.

senses.definition: Opposing communism or communist parties.
examples: He made a series of anticommunist speeches.
The government led an anticommunist campaign.
Anticommunist laws were passed during that period.

senses.definition: A person who opposes communism.
examples: She is a well‑known anticommunist in the region.
Many anticommunists supported the new party.
The group brought together liberals, conservatives, and anticommunists.

senses.definition: Having ideas or actions that oppose communism.
examples: The group follows an anticommunistic ideology.
He wrote an anticommunistic pamphlet in the 1950s.
Their anticommunistic position shaped foreign policy.

senses.definition: Old‑fashioned form meaning strongly opposed to communism.
examples: The document reflects an anticommunistical mindset.
He delivered an anticommunistical sermon in the church.
Such anticommunistical language was common at the time.

senses.definition: In a way that shows strong opposition to communism.
examples: The party reacted anticommunistically to the proposal.
He wrote anticommunistically throughout his career.
The government spoke anticommunistically in international forums.

senses.definition: Plural of anticommunist: people who oppose communism.
examples: Anticommunists organized protests against the new regime.
Many anticommunists fled the country after the revolution.
Liberals and conservatives joined anticommunists in the coalition.

senses.definition: Describing an operation where changing the order of the elements changes the sign or gives a related but different result, such as $$ab = -ba$$.
examples: In some algebraic structures, the product is anticommutative.
The bracket operation is anticommutative by definition.
These variables obey anticommutative rules in the theory.
